Costumed Saoirse Review - Empires & Puzzles The Vanquisher Guide

The Vanquisher has arrived, and she's not just here to look pretty in plate mail. Costumed Saoirse brings modern stats, a nasty bleed, and a mana engine that ramps up faster than ever. Is she the update the Slayer family desperately needed?
Costumed Saoirse: The Blood-Soaked Knight Rises
Costumed Saoirse: The Vanquisher Who Outlasts Everyone
The Slayers of Fell Shadows were always a "slow burn" family, but

(The Vanquisher) pours gasoline on that fire. Released as a major update to the 2021 classic, this costume transforms her from a fragile defense-down specialist into a Paladin tank-buster with incredible self-sustain. She keeps the core Slayer identity—start slow, end fast—but buffs the numbers to 2024/2026 standards. If you like heroes who get stronger the longer you ignore them, you're in for a treat.

Gone are the barbarian leathers; Costumed Saoirse is decked out in full, gleaming red plate armor. She looks like a proper Knight Commander now, dignified and dangerous. The "Vanquisher" title fits the upgrade perfectly—she looks less like a wild brawler and more like an unstoppable force of organized destruction. It's a fantastic glow-up that signals her increased durability and Paladin class.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The updated Slayer family bonus (if you have the costume bonus active) makes the stacks apply more consistently. The ability to gain mana and health passively at the end of turns is what allows her to "snowball."
A nice bit of chip damage. It won't wipe a team, but it softens them up and cancels out enemy healing over time.
Still excellent. Reducing burn/poison damage by 50% effectively doubles her health pool against those archetypes.
This fits the costume perfectly. Since she needs time to build stacks, taking less damage helps her survive the crucial first few turns.
The Slayers were feared for their snowball potential but mocked for their initial slowness. The costume fixes the "reward" part of the risk/reward equation.

Costumed Saoirse is fully modernized. Her stats are competitive with 2025/2026 releases. The Slayer mechanic, which was once "too slow," is now a viable counter to "stalemate" teams because she eventually overpowers them.
She is excellent in wars and raids where you can afford a slightly slower start. She is a top-tier duelist who can win 1v3 situations once fully stacked.

The best hero for climbing towers. Her stacks carry her through grueling waves.

Equalizer cleanses stacks? No, stacks usually resist cleanses, but check current mechanics. If stacks persist, she's good. If War Equalizer removes stacks (it usually removes status effects, stacks are distinct), she is fine.
Note: Stacks typically persist through Equalizer.
A scary flank. If the attacker gets a bad board, she punishes them by becoming unkillable.
Putting her at flank allows her to survive. Pairing with another Slayer (like Senan) increases the passive stack chance, making the whole team ramp up faster.
EDD from Marjana + DD from Saoirse = Deleted enemies.
As always, stopping her mana stops her stacks.
Prevents the regen buff if it's considered a buff (stacks are usually separate, but the regen effect might be blocked).
This is a "carry" hero. Even if your other heroes are dead, a fully stacked Saoirse can solo the rest of the enemy team. Level her immediately.
She is a luxury tool for wars and towers. The Costume Bonus stats alone make her worth the mats. She is a great counter to "stall" teams because she eventually out-stalls them.

Costumed Saoirse is the definition of "It's a marathon, not a sprint." Except she cheats and brings a motorcycle to the marathon after the first mile. She is a beautifully designed hero that rewards patience and survival with overwhelming power. While the meta favors speed, Saoirse proves that sometimes, being the last woman standing is all that matters.
A powerhouse for modern PvP and a must-have for anyone who enjoys watching their hero turn into a raid boss.
A comprehensive review of Costumed Saoirse (The Vanquisher). We break down her updated stats, Bleed passive, and why she might be the best long-battle hero in the Fire element for 2026.
